PDS Objective-C Implementation Guide

layout: default title: PDS Objective-C Implementation Guide

PDS Objective-C Implementation Guide

Welcome to the comprehensive documentation guide for implementing an ATProto Personal Data Server (PDS) in Objective-C. This guide bridges the gap between high-level architecture concepts and practical implementation details, enabling developers to understand and build production-grade PDS systems.

What is a PDS?

A Personal Data Server (PDS) is a core component of the AT Protocol ecosystem. It stores and manages user data (posts, profiles, relationships) in a decentralized manner, giving users control over their information while maintaining protocol compliance.

Why Objective-C?

This implementation targets both macOS and Linux/GNUstep, leveraging Objective-C's: - Strong runtime introspection capabilities - Excellent memory management with ARC - Native integration with macOS frameworks - Cross-platform compatibility via GNUstep

Quick Navigation

  • Overview — What is a PDS and why Objective-C
  • Architecture Overview — High-level system diagram
  • Setup — Build environment and dependencies

  • AT Protocol Basics — DID, NSID, fundamentals
  • CBOR and CAR — Serialization formats
  • Merkle Search Trees — Data structure
  • Cryptography — JWT, DPoP, ECDSA P-256

  • PDSApplication Facade — Main application class

  • Account Service — User management
  • Record Service — Data operations
  • Blob Service — File storage
  • Repository Service — MST management
  • Admin Service — Moderation
  • Relay Service — External notifications

  • HTTP Server — Custom HTTP implementation
  • XRPC Dispatch — RPC routing
  • Method Registry — Endpoint registration
  • Domain Methods — Handler patterns
  • Auth Helpers — JWT/DPoP verification
  • Error Handling — Standardized responses

  • SQLite Architecture — Design patterns
  • Service Databases — Shared DB
  • Actor Databases — Per-user pools
  • Migrations — Schema versioning
  • WAL Mode — Write-Ahead Logging

  • OAuth 2.0 with DPoP — OAuth flow
  • Key Rotation — Key management
  • TOTP and WebAuthn — MFA

  • Repository Basics — Structure
  • CBOR Serialization — Encoding
  • CAR Format — Archive format
  • CID and Hashing — Content addressing
  • Blob Storage — File management

  • WebSocket Server — Connection handling
  • Commit Broadcasting — Event streaming
  • Backpressure — Flow control

  • Compatibility Layer — Abstraction
  • Network Transport — I/O
  • ARC Runtime — Memory management

  • Tutorial 4: Authentication — OAuth/JWT

  • Config Reference — Configuration
  • CLI Reference — kaszlak commands

Learning Path

For New Developers: 1. Start with Getting Started 2. Read Core Concepts 3. Follow Tutorial 1: Hello PDS 4. Explore Application Layer

For Feature Implementation: 1. Review Architecture Overview 2. Read relevant service documentation 3. Check Network Layer for endpoints 4. Follow corresponding tutorial

For Troubleshooting: 1. Check Troubleshooting Guide 2. Review Error Handling 3. Check Platform Compatibility

Documentation Structure

This guide is organized into 12 progressive sections, each building on previous knowledge:

  • Sections 1-2: Foundation and core concepts
  • Sections 3-6: Implementation layers (application, network, database, auth)
  • Sections 7-9: Advanced topics (protocol, sync, compatibility)
  • Sections 10-12: Practical guides (tutorials, reference, diagrams)

Each section includes: - Conceptual overview - Architecture diagrams - Code examples from the actual codebase - Implementation patterns - Best practices - Common pitfalls

Code Examples

All code examples in this guide are extracted from the actual PDS codebase and tested to ensure they compile and run correctly. Examples include: - Line references to source files - Syntax highlighting - Error handling patterns - Real-world usage

Diagrams

Visual representations help understand complex flows: - System architecture showing all components - Request flow from client to database - Database schema and relationships - Authentication flows - Firehose event broadcasting

Getting Help

  • Questions about architecture? See Architecture Overview
  • Need to implement a feature? Check the relevant service documentation
  • Stuck on a problem? See Troubleshooting
  • Want to learn by doing? Follow the Tutorials

Contributing

This documentation is maintained alongside the codebase. When making changes to the PDS implementation: 1. Update relevant documentation sections 2. Update code examples if patterns change 3. Update diagrams if architecture changes 4. Run documentation validation checks

Last Updated: 2024 Version: 1.0 Status: In Development
